entries.definition:
z-r-q blue, to become blue; to pass through swiftly, to pass excrement. The term أَزْرَق ʾazraq, which is usually translated as ‘blue’, in fact covers a range of colours in Arabic including grey, green, and white and also means purity of water. Of this root, only زُرْق zurq occurs once in the Qur’an.
زُرْق zurq [pl. of n. أَزْرَق ʾazraq] blue, grey, green, white-eyed (with terror); sightless (20:102) يَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ وَنَحْشُرُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ زُرْقًا the day the Trumpet will be sounded and We gather the sinful, sightless.

زُرْق zurq [pl. of n. أَزْرَق ʾazraq] blue, grey, green, white-eyed (with terror); sightless (20:102) يَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ وَنَحْشُرُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ زُرْقًا the day the Trumpet will be sounded and We gather the sinful, sightless.
